title: fetch_url response body is not size-capped
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/web.rs:262-300 (fetch_url)
## Violation
After validating the URL, the response body is consumed via `response.text().await` (line 285) with no maximum size. A malicious or compromised Jina-proxied target can stream gigabytes, exhausting memory before the post-fetch truncate-to-50000 (`src/tools/web.rs:47`) ever runs. The truncate logic in the tool layer is not a defense — it executes on an already fully-materialized `String`.

```rust
let content = response
    .text()
    .await
    .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to read response: {e}"))?;
```

Same exposure in `fetch_with_browser` via headless Chrome `get_content()`.
## Mandated refactor
Stream the body with `response.bytes_stream()` and accumulate into a capped buffer (e.g. 4 MiB), returning an error when the cap is exceeded. Alternatively check `response.content_length()` upfront and refuse early. For the headless-browser path, set a navigation-level body limit or kill the tab when content_size > cap.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
